assertionResults.title: Family commands honor strict scope routing

steps.action: Run skills|rules|hooks|subagents with -y -n (default project scope) and with --user using isolated HOME
steps.expected: Default routes to project scope; --user routes to HOME scope only
steps.observed: rules showed project-only language detection vs user global mode; subagents DRY-RUN destinations switched from repo path to HOME with --user; hooks showed deterministic no-hook message in both scopes; skills user run showed HOME DRY-RUN destinations when rulesync execution was enabled.

skills_project_noskip: [rulesync] ℹ ✓ All files are up to date (skills)
hooks_project: ℹ️  No hook scripts found (add .sh files to hooks/)
hooks_user: ℹ️  No hook scripts found (add .sh files to hooks/)

assertionResults.title: Multi-agent selection uses deterministic union behavior

steps.action: Compare subagents -y -n outputs for repeated --agent flags, comma list, and reverse+duplicate order
steps.expected: Deduplicated deterministic union with equivalent outputs
steps.observed: All three variants exited 0 and produced byte-identical output (same warning + same DRY-RUN subagent lines).

r1: ⚠️  Skipping subagents for factorydroid: subagents require .claude/agents/ which is not supported by factorydroid
r2: ⚠️  Skipping subagents for factorydroid: subagents require .claude/agents/ which is not supported by factorydroid
r3: ⚠️  Skipping subagents for factorydroid: subagents require .claude/agents/ which is not supported by factorydroid

steps.action: Run invalid agent values on rules and skills dry-run commands
steps.expected: Fail fast with non-zero exit and clear guidance including valid agents
steps.observed: Both commands exited 1 and printed explicit invalid-agent errors with valid target list.

rules_invalid_agent: ❌ Invalid agent target(s): "invalid-agent". Valid agents: claudecode, codexcli, factorydroid
skills_invalid_agents: ❌ Invalid agent target(s): "bad1", "bad2". Valid agents: claudecode, codexcli, factorydroid

assertionResults.title: Fully unsupported selection becomes warning-only no-op

steps.action: Run fully unsupported agent/artifact combinations for hooks and subagents in dry-run mode
steps.expected: Warnings only, no writes, success exit
steps.observed: Commands emitted skipping warnings + "Nothing to install", produced no DRY-RUN hook lines, exited 0, and HOME snapshot remained unchanged.

⚠️  Skipping hooks for factorydroid: hooks require .claude/hooks/ which is not supported by factorydroid
⚠️  Nothing to install — all requested combinations are unsupported.

⚠️  Nothing to install — all requested combinations are unsupported.

⚠️  Nothing to install — all requested combinations are unsupported.

frictions.description: With AF_SKIP_RULESYNC_EXEC=1, `skills -y -n` produced no DRY-RUN destination lines, so strict scope destination evidence for skills required two additional dry-run checks without AF_SKIP_RULESYNC_EXEC.
frictions.resolution: Re-ran `skills -y -n` and `skills -y -n --user` (still dry-run) to capture concrete destination paths under HOME for --user scope.

frictions.description: `hooks` family has no installable .sh artifacts in this repo, so both scopes return informational no-hook messages instead of destination DRY-RUN lines.
frictions.resolution: Used deterministic exit codes/messages for hooks and relied on rules/subagents/skills scope evidence for destination routing checks.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 4 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Scope routing, agent union determinism, invalid-agent failures, and fully unsupported no-op behavior all matched expected CLI contract behavior in isolated HOME `/tmp/af-utv-install-scoping-u3/home`.
